info.name: MDM MVP - Customer Management
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for testing the MDM (Master Data Management) MVP system with CQRS architecture.

## Architecture
- **Customer Ingestion Service** (port 8080) — Command side (writes via Kafka)
- **Customer Mastering Service** (port 8081) — Query side (reads from PostgreSQL)
- **OAuth2 Server** (port 9999) — JWT token issuance (RS256)
- **Kafka** (port 9092) — Event streaming (KRaft mode)
- **PostgreSQL** (port 5432) — Golden record storage

## Key Concepts
- **nationalId** is the canonical unique identifier for customer deduplication
- Deduplication is by **nationalId exact match** (not email)
- Kafka partition key = nationalId (guarantees per-customer ordering)
- Dual-key idempotency: client-provided + SHA-256(nationalId|sourceSystem)
- DLQ (Dead Letter Queue) for poison messages after retry exhaustion

## Collection Structure
1. **OAuth2 Authentication** — Get JWT tokens
2. **Health & Setup** — Service health checks
3. **Command Side (Write)** — Customer ingestion with nationalId
4. **Query Side (Read)** — Customer queries by nationalId
5. **Idempotency Testing** — Duplicate detection
6. **Validation & Errors** — Error handling
7. **DLQ & Retry** — Dead Letter Queue testing
8. **Observability** — Metrics & SLOs

## Environment Variables
- `base_url_ingestion`: http://localhost:8080
- `base_url_mastering`: http://localhost:8081
- `oauth_url`: http://localhost:9999
- `oauth_token`: JWT token (auto-set)
- `test_national_id`: Test national ID (auto-set)
- `test_customer_id`: Golden record ID (auto-set)

item.name: 00 - OAuth2 Authentication

request.description: Get JWT token for admin user (full access: ADMIN, CUSTOMER_WRITE, CUSTOMER_READ). Uses RS256 algorithm.
Get JWT token for regular user (write access only: CUSTOMER_WRITE)
Get JWT token for read-only user (read access only: CUSTOMER_READ)
Get JWT token using client credentials flow (machine-to-machine)
Get current user information from OAuth2 server
Get OAuth2/OpenID Connect configuration. Shows token endpoint, JWKS URI, and supported grant types.
Get the RSA public keys used by Spring Boot to validate JWT tokens (RS256 algorithm).

item.description: OAuth2 authentication and token management. Run these first to get JWT tokens for API requests. Uses RS256 (RSA) algorithm.

request.description: Shows all health indicators: database, kafka, mdmProcessing, diskSpace, liveness, readiness.

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description:
Ingest first customer by nationalId. Returns 202 Accepted (async processing). Requires OAuth2 token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope.

nationalId (12-13 alphanumeric chars) is the canonical unique identifier used for deduplication.

Wait 3-5 seconds for Kafka processing to complete before running queries.

request.description: Ingest customer from CRM source system.

Search customers by name (supports partial matching).

item.name: Check Customer Exists By National ID

request.description: Verify that a non-existent nationalId returns exists=false.

exec: console.log("Duplicate submitted with same nationalId - should be detected by mastering service");

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description:
Submit duplicate with SAME nationalId but different name/email/phone.
The mastering service will detect this as a duplicate via nationalId exact match and UPDATE the existing golden record (coalesce merge strategy).

exec: console.log("Idempotent replay detected - same nationalId + sourceSystem returns cached eventId");

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description:
Submit the EXACT same nationalId + sourceSystem combination.

The ingestion service will detect this via the deterministic idempotency key SHA-256(nationalId|sourceSystem) and return 200 OK with status=CACHED (not 202 ACCEPTED).

First submission → 202 ACCEPTED
Replay → 200 OK (CACHED, with X-Idempotency-Replay: true header)

header.description: OAuth2 access token with CUSTOMER_WRITE scope

request.description:
Submit with a client-provided X-Idempotency-Key header.

Replaying the same key will return the cached result (200 OK, status=CACHED) instead of creating a duplicate event.

TTL: 24 hours for client-provided keys.

Query the golden record after duplicate submission.
The version should be incremented and fields merged via coalesce strategy.
nationalId is required. Should return 400 Bad Request.
nationalId must be 12-13 alphanumeric characters. Should return 400.

exec: pm.test("Error mentions valid source systems", function() {

request.description: sourceSystem must be one of: BANK, GOVERNMENT, CRM, MOBILE, WEB, PARTNER. Should return 400.
Missing Content-Type header should return 415 Unsupported Media Type.
Missing OAuth2 token should return 401 Unauthorized.

item.name: Get Non-Existent Customer By National ID (404)

request.description: Get customer by non-existent nationalId should return 404.

List all Kafka topics. Expected topics:
- customer.raw
- customer.mastered
- customer.dlq

Run this from the terminal, not Postman. Use: docker-compose exec kafka kafka-topics --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 --list

url.raw: docker-compose exec kafka kafka-topics --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 --describe --topic customer.dlq

request.description:
Describe the DLQ topic configuration (partitions, replication factor, retention).

Run from terminal: docker-compose exec kafka kafka-topics --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 --describe --topic customer.dlq

url.raw: docker-compose exec kafka kafka-console-consumer --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 --topic customer.dlq --from-beginning --max-messages 10 --timeout-ms 10000

request.description:
Consume DLQ messages to inspect failed events.

Run from terminal:
docker-compose exec kafka kafka-console-consumer --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 --topic customer.dlq --from-beginning --max-messages 10 --timeout-ms 10000

DLQ messages contain:
- originalEvent: the failed CustomerRawEvent
- errorDetails: exception, message, stackTrace
- processingHistory: retry attempts
- schemaVersion: v1

response.name: DLQ Message Example

request.description:
Check mastering service logs for retry attempts and DLQ messages.

Run from terminal:
docker-compose logs --tail=100 customer-mastering-service

Look for:
- 'Retry attempt for event' — retry in progress
- 'Message sent to DLQ' — all retries exhausted
- 'Successfully processed event' — normal processing

Filter logs for DLQ-related entries.

Run from terminal:
docker-compose logs --tail=50 customer-mastering-service | grep -i DLQ

NOTE: This is a placeholder. DLQ reprocessing typically requires a separate consumer or manual Kafka producer.

To manually reprocess a DLQ message:
1. Extract the originalEvent from the DLQ message
2. Publish it back to customer.raw topic:
   docker-compose exec kafka kafka-console-producer --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 --topic customer.raw
3. Paste the originalEvent JSON

Or build a DLQ reprocessor service that:
- Reads from customer.dlq
- Validates/fixes the data
- Republishes to customer.raw

DLQ Event Structure (com.mdm.mastering.dto.dlq.DlqEvent):

{
  "originalEvent": { /* CustomerRawEvent */ },
  "errorDetails": {
    "exception": "ExceptionClassName",
    "message": "Error message (truncated to 500 chars)",
    "stackTrace": "Full stack trace"
  },
  "processingHistory": [
    {
      "attempt": 3,
      "timestamp": "2026-04-03T20:30:05Z",
      "error": "Error message (truncated to 200 chars)"
    }
  ],
  "schemaVersion": "v1"
}

Error Types:
- TRANSIENT: Retryable (DB deadlock, timeout) → retried 3 times with exponential backoff
- PERMANENT: Non-retryable (constraint violation, invalid data) → goes directly to DLQ
- BUSINESS: Business rule failures → logged and skipped

Retry Configuration:
- maxAttempts: 3
- initialInterval: 1000ms
- maxInterval: 10000ms
- multiplier: 2.0

Get detailed health status including processing metrics:
- totalProcessed: total events processed
- totalDuplicates: duplicates detected
- duplicateRate: percentage of duplicates

SLO Target: 99.9% availability
Get error count for burn rate calculation.

Get latency histogram for p50/p95/p99 calculation.

SLO: 99% of events processed in < 1 second.

Get deduplication lookup latency.

SLO: 99% of lookups < 10ms (nationalId indexed).

item.name: 09 - Interview Demo Script

request.description: ✅ Show all services are UP (DB, Kafka, Processing).
✅ Get admin JWT token (RS256 signed).

item.name: Demo Step 3: Ingest Customer (New Golden Record)

request.description: ✅ Ingest customer by nationalId → returns 202 Accepted (async processing).
✅ Query golden record by nationalId — wait 3 seconds after ingestion.
✅ Submit duplicate (same nationalId, different data) → mastering service detects duplicate and updates golden record.

item.name: Demo Step 6: Verify Updated Golden Record

request.description: ✅ Show golden record was updated (version incremented, fields merged via coalesce).
✅ Show Prometheus metrics: events_processed_total, duplicates_detected_total, golden_records_created_total.

item.description: Step-by-step interview demo. Run these requests in order to showcase the MDM system.
